text.text:
I love to eat at Kura Revolving Sushi Bar in Berkeley! I come frequently, as it is definitely a favorite for me, my friends, and my family.

It’s a fun, modern place to eat. The dining area is clean and sunny (with big windows letting the Berkeley sunshine stream in), and I find the conveyor belt that carries the sushi plates and the robot that brings the water and wasabi to the table so cute!

I like how you order by the plate (most plates are $4.10), which means you can have as much or as little as you can afford or feel hungry for. I’ve come here for just a plate or two before, and I’ve also come with friends and accumulated tall stacks of plates. (If you order enough plates, you might even win cute prizes that are dispensed from above the ordering console at every table!)

The environment and concept are wonderful, but the food here speaks for itself. When it comes to sushi, there’s a variety of choices that range from traditional to modern. The fish and meat are always fresh, the rice is always perfectly sticky and well-seasoned, and the nori is always crisp—especially if you order on the console because then it will come straight to your table from the kitchen on a conveyor belt. I particularly recommend the tekkamkai and the negitoro gunkan; I try these two plates almost every time I come, and I come a lot. The wasabi, soy sauce, and ginger are free and high-quality. I love to use plenty of them with my sushi.

There’s also plenty of great non-sushi items, including ramen, udon, and even fries. The miso soup here is warm and soothing, a perfect appetizer, and the beef udon is also a favorite of mine. (I love the slurpy, chewy noodles and the warm broth with the tender beef).

They take dessert as seriously as the main course. You can’t go wrong here, but for dessert, I recommend the warabi mochi, the sesame balls, and the gooey butter cake. Personally, I often find it hard to choose just one.

The menu is not set in stone, and they frequently circulate exciting specials on and off the menu, so it’s usually worth it to browse the entire menu before ordering. I think my favorite special item that’s ever been on the menu is the negitoro maki roll. (Maybe they'll bring it back, but I don't know.)

They also change out their cute prizes, if you’re into that. I have some cute key chains from when we ordered a bunch of plates a while back.

I downloaded their app, so I can receive discounts/coupons from time to time.

Eating here is always a treat!

text.text: Made my kids happy with the conveyor and prize experience. I did like the plate disposal experience. Overall, I was disappointed with food, with ramen being the biggest disappointment. The pork slices were not fresh, very chewy, and didn’t taste right. Overall not the best value for portion sizes. If you like gimmicks, this is the place for you!

originalText.text: Made my kids happy with the conveyor and prize experience. I did like the plate disposal experience. Overall, I was disappointed with food, with ramen being the biggest disappointment. The pork slices were not fresh, very chewy, and didn’t taste right. Overall not the best value for portion sizes. If you like gimmicks, this is the place for you!

text.text:
Parking is a little hard to find. Only street parkings.

Place: it's a chain restaurant. This place is clean and updated. Inside is clean and good customer service.

FOOD: food choices are same as other locations. Before I came to this location, I thought food are the same quality across all restaurants. BUT it's not!!! I don't know why food at this location is so so so much better than other two locations I have tried. It's fresh!

Some of the pictures I took is not great. But I would come again!

text.text: My daughter wanted to come here for lunch to try the noodles. I like the choices they have on the revolving belt. There is also a side menu to order from.

originalText.text: My daughter wanted to come here for lunch to try the noodles. I like the choices they have on the revolving belt. There is also a side menu to order from.

text.text: Fun experience to dine here. Sushi was okay but mostly it’s the experience that matters here. Theres serving robot, unique ordering experience as well as the way you can return dishes at the table.
